_comment: Copy to config.json next to server.py and edit values. Or point RETRY_WATCHDOG_CONFIG env var at a private path.

server._webhook_url_comment: Optional override for the URL that the Admin view considers 'this watchdog's webhook'. Use it when the daemon binds an internal port that's fronted by a portproxy (e.g. WSL2 bind 9094, externally reachable at 9095). Empty = auto-compute as http://localhost:<port>/webhook.

alert._comment: default_recipient is used when a cron has no per-cron alert_recipient set. sender_binary must be a CLI that accepts --account/--to/--subject/--body (e.g. gog-send).

sender_env._comment: Extra env vars passed to the sender subprocess. WSL2 + gog-send needs SSL_CERT_FILE.

heartbeat._comment: Background scanner config. Runs every interval_minutes, iterates each cron with predicates configured, and evaluates them against the latest status=ok run found in runs_dir_path/<cron_id>.jsonl. Failed predicates trigger the same retry-or-alert flow as a webhook failure. lookback_hours bounds how far back the scanner considers runs (prevents re-evaluating very old runs after a restart). jobs_json_path is read by the Admin view + AI suggestion plane to look up each cron's prompt + configured model. Missed-run detection (firing on crons that never ran at all) is NOT yet implemented; see Roadmap in the README.

_openclaw_instance_name_comment: Optional label shown in the UI header (e.g. 'C3PO', 'prod-east'). Distinguishes a watchdog instance from its hostname when you're running multiple watchdogs across machines. Leave empty to hide.

predicates._comment: v0.2 — per-cron predicate rules. After each successful run (status=ok), the heartbeat scanner evaluates every predicate; the FIRST failed predicate triggers the same retry-or-alert flow as a webhook failure. Catches the case where the cron reports status=ok but didn't actually do its work. Key is cron UUID.
predicates._path_placeholders: {TODAY} and {YESTERDAY} resolve to YYYY-MM-DD in server.timezone

00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000.description: Output file must have been written within 30 min and be at least 100 bytes
At least one record has a non-null result
At least one pick written by the daily-sports-report source
Rolling log file must have grown since last scan

ai._comment: Optional AI Assist plane (v0.4+). When enabled in Settings and a primary model is picked, three features become available: (1) Suggest with AI button in the predicate editor (v0.4); (2) Suggest with AI button in the healthcheck editor (v0.5); (3) Failure-mode explanations injected into alert emails AND available on-demand via the History modal's Explain button (v0.6). Models are picked from openclaw.json's configured providers; per-model tuning is auto-resolved from the built-in registry. Default is disabled (opt-in).
ai._max_tokens_comment: Default token budget for predicate / healthcheck suggestion calls. Capped at the model's own maxTokens from openclaw.json at call time.
ai._timeout_seconds_comment: Per-call wall-clock for predicate / healthcheck suggestions. These run on operator click — slowness is tolerable.
ai._explain_timeout_seconds_comment: Per-call wall-clock for inline alert-time failure-mode diagnoses (v0.6). Tight by design — a slow or offline model must not delay the alert email. Hard cap of 15s enforced in code regardless of the configured value.
ai._explain_max_tokens_comment: Token budget for failure-mode diagnosis responses (v0.6). Diagnoses are short by spec (cause + next_step + confidence + category), so 512 is comfortable.
ai._tunings_comment: Per-model tuning overrides. Different model families need different knobs to produce clean JSON output. The built-in registry in ai.py (BUILTIN_TUNINGS) covers Qwen2/3, GPT-OSS, DeepSeek, GLM, Gemma, Mistral, Nemotron via pattern matching on model_id. Overrides here win in resolution order: exact model_key match (provider/model_id) -> exact model_id match -> built-in pattern -> default. Each tuning may set: extra_body (dict merged into request body), temperature, max_tokens, system_prompt_prefix (text prepended to user message). See GET /api/ai/tunings for the active registry.

healthchecks._comment: Per-cron pre-retry healthcheck rules (v0.5). Before the watchdog retries a failed cron, every configured healthcheck for that cron is evaluated. If ANY healthcheck fails, the retry is SKIPPED — the watchdog assumes a dependency is down (model server, upstream API, network split) and emits a dependency-unhealthy alert with the failing check's details. The retry-count budget is NOT consumed (a down dependency isn't the cron's fault). Same predicate-type schema as the predicates section above (file_mtime, file_grew, json_field_count, http_health). The AI Suggest with AI button in the healthcheck editor proposes rules with explicit anti-patterns (no checks on script files, config files, the cron's own output, etc. — only checks on external services that must be RESPONDING for a retry to have any chance of succeeding).

00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000.description: Upstream model server must be responding
